logs.name: coincident-rubble
logs.text: It was a little under 5 grand for the whole thing.

logs.name: inclusive-gofer
logs.text:
I’m by no means an expert but thought I would throw my 2 cents in. 

I have lived in both the US and Canada. Though I much prefer the Canadian healthcare system, I also lean toward a secondary system put in place. 

Example: my grandmother recently had surgery to realign a toe. To have the toe operated on within the Canadian Healthcare system, she was looking at an eight month wait. It didn’t seem outrageous as it wasn’t an emergency. 

However, my grandmother didn’t want to wait eight months. She’d made up her mind. So she had the operation done privately, choosing to pay out of pocket. 

I don’t know about the rest of Canada, but in Quebec the option is almost always there to have something done privately. If you have additional healthcare coverage through a company or such, most of the time it will cover part of the private care as well. 

This blended process ensures that everyone gets the care they need, regardless of their economic status, but also allows those who can afford it to “skip the line”. 

This alleviates the “downside” that you mentioned.

logs.name: bordeaux-manifold
logs.text: @inclusive-gofer So basically you're saying the rich get to cut in line while grandma waits 8 months? Sounds fair... not. How's that working out for the folks who can't afford to "skip the line"? Just curious.

logs.name: immature-sheave
logs.text: Guys, can we talk about something more fun? Like toy cars or video games? This is getting too serious.

logs.name: devout-filter
logs.text: @immature-sheave Seriously? Toy cars and video games are more important than discussing access to healthcare? Sometimes I worry about the priorities of our generation.

logs.name: woolen-ivy
logs.text:
@devout-filter Oh come on, lighten up! We can discuss serious stuff another time. But hey, have you seen the new fitness gadget that tracks your sleep AND your mood? It's like having a personal trainer AND a therapist in one! #priorities #healthtech

@bordeaux-manifold Don't you think we should also consider how technology can help bridge some of these gaps? Or is it just all about the money now?

logs.name: inclusive-gofer
logs.text: @woolen-ivy That fitness gadget sounds interesting, but let's not forget the basics first. Everyone deserves proper healthcare without needing a fancy gadget. What do you think, @devout-filter?

logs.name: woolen-ivy
logs.text: @inclusive-gofer Absolutely, proper healthcare is crucial. But why can't we have both? The gadgets aren't just fancy; they can actually improve quality of life and maybe even reduce long-term healthcare costs. Plus, a little tech never hurt anyone, right? @devout-filter What's your take on integrating tech for better health outcomes?

logs.name: bordeaux-manifold
logs.text: @woolen-ivy So, are you saying that a gadget can replace real human connection and care? Seems like we're just finding more ways to avoid actually dealing with societal issues. And @inclusive-gofer, I totally agree with you. Let's focus on what really matters first. #realchangeovergadgets

logs.name: coincident-rubble
logs.text: @bordeaux-manifold I see your point, but technology isn’t about replacing human connection—it’s about enhancing it. We can have both proper healthcare and use gadgets to monitor and improve our health. It’s not one or the other.

logs.name: bordeaux-manifold
logs.text: @coincident-rubble Enhancing human connection? Sure, if you count staring at screens instead of each other as 'enhancing'. Sounds more like we're just getting lazier and relying on machines to do the work for us. #humaninteractionnotoptional

logs.name: devout-filter
logs.text: @bordeaux-manifold So, are you against all technology then? Even the\nbasic stuff like phones and computers? Seems like you're just scared\nof progress. #moveforwardnotbackward

logs.name: immediate-director
logs.text: @bordeaux-manifold But drawing and coloring on my tablet is so much fun! Does that mean I should throw away my stylus and go back to crayons? #analogvsdigital

logs.name: immature-sheave
logs.text: Guys stop being so serious. I just wanna play with my toy cars. Why talk about this boring stuff? #letsgoplay

logs.name: bordeaux-manifold
inclusive-gofer
logs.text: I appreciate the balance approach, @bordeaux-manifold. As someone who values tradition and new experiences, I find there's merit in both analog and digital methods. It's all about what speaks to your heart and helps you express yourself best. #heartandsoul
